channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Fiancée of Faster Pussycat singer dies after going overboard on cruise, family says
item.description: Her husband-to-be, Faster Pussycat singer Taime Downe, was among the musical acts slated to perform on the cruise.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: US Coast Guard intercepts boat carrying Russian nationals, criminal migrant
item.description: A Coast Guard cutter intercepted five migrants attempting to travel illegally into the U.S. in the Trump administration's renewed efforts to combat illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: what’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the brand at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday. Here’s what Taco Bell fans can expect on menus in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration deletes list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it had identified to close or sell, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fiancée of Faster Pussycat singer dies after going overboard on '80s-themed cruise, family says
item.description: Kimberly Burch, the fiancée of an '80s rock star, has died after going overboard an '80s-themed cruise, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KREX 5

item.title: Ohio home that exploded was pending sale: Fire chief
item.description: The hot water heater and furnace were part of the sale, but all the home's appliances were capped, Barberton Fire Chief Stephen Ionno said.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Trudeau not willing to lift Canada's retaliatory tariffs if Trump leaves some tariffs on Canada
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau is unwilling to lift Canada’s retaliatory tariffs on the United States if President Donald Trump leaves any U.S. tariffs on Canada, a senior government official told The Associated Press on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Veterans are speaking out on the Trump administration's plans to cut the VA's budget
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Stephen Watson served in the Marines for 22 years and receives care through the Department of Veterans Affairs for a traumatic brain injury. He supports President Donald Trump and adviser Elon Musk's cost-cutting program — even if it affects the VA.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lava fountain height soars in latest episode of Hawaii volcano eruption
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Lava fountains from a Hawaii volcano reached heights of 600 feet (180 meters) during the latest episode of an eruption that has been pausing and resuming for several months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Neighbors concerned after hidden camera found outside home
item.description: “A deputy searched the area around the home and discovered a concealed camera in a bush near the house,” the sheriff's department said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Avalanche buries at least 3 skiers near Anchorage, Alaska officials say
item.description: Alaska State Troopers say three skiers who had flown by helicopter to a remote mountain range were swept away by an avalanche and likely killed under the weight of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court denies Trump administration request to cancel $2 billion in foreign aid
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 emergency ruling Wednesday refused to halt a judge’s decision ordering the Trump administration to immediately release nearly $2 billion in foreign aid payments owed under existing contracts.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Ash Wednesday: What you should know about the start of the season
item.description: DAYTON, Ohio (WDTN) – What is Ash Wednesday? Ash Wednesday 2025, which falls on March 5 this year, marks the official beginning of the Christian season of Lent, which culminates with Easter on April 20, 2025. The 46-day season is meant for penitence, sacrifice, and reflection.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation into Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey that gathers near real-time data about American life.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, Fox 5

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chipotle will absorb tariff-related cost increases, CEO says
item.description: With the Trump administration's 25% tariff on all Mexican imports set to take effect Tuesday, Chipotle has pledged to absorb potential cost increases.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Crews from across South Carolina help battle Carolina Forest-area wildfires
item.description: Regional and state fire rescue crews on Monday continued to battle the Carolina Forest-area wildfires, according to Horry County Fire Rescue.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, Fox 5

item.title: This rare bean may be your future coffee as warming climate hurts crops
item.description: As leading coffee-producing countries struggle to grow crops in drier, less reliable weather, prices have soared to the highest in decades and the industry is scrambling for solutions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NOAA firings spark fears about long-term damage
item.description: The Trump administration has made drastic cuts to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) that threaten to impact weather forecasting and other key services provided by the agency.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Daryl Hannah voices support for Ukraine on Oscars stage
item.description: The 64-year-old performer's brief remark came days after an explosive confrontation between President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Oscars Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: Hollywood's biggest stars and most talented artists are set to be honored Sunday evening during the 97th Academy Awards held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. But before the awards ceremony itself begins, the stars are walking the iconic Red Carpet where fashion takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, WGN 9
